Projeto 4: Campo Magnético¶

Caderno de apresentação do Projeto 4 de Eletromagnetismo Computacional.

In [1]:
import numpy as np
import sympy as sp
from scipy import integrate
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from mpl_toolkits.mplot3d import Axes3D
In [2]:
from ipywidgets import interactive, IntSlider
import ipywidgets as widgets
In [3]:
plt.style.use('em-comp')
In [4]:
from project4 import Grid, MagneticField, FieldSlicer
In [5]:
from project4 import generate_colormap

Hélice¶

Parametrização¶

In [6]:
delta, epsilon = sp.symbols(r'delta epsilon', constants=True)
In [7]:
t = sp.symbols('t', real=True)
In [8]:
curr_elem1 = sp.Matrix([[delta*sp.cos(t), delta*sp.sin(t), epsilon*t/(2*sp.pi)]])
In [9]:
curr_elem_der1 = sp.diff(curr_elem1, t)

Gerando a malha¶

In [10]:
grid1 = Grid([-1.0, 1.1, 0.2], [-1.0, 1.1, 0.2], [-0.2, 1.3, 0.1])
In [11]:
delta1, epsilon1 = 1.0, 1.0
In [12]:
grid1.plot_curve(curr_elem1, 0, 2*np.pi/epsilon1, args='t, delta=%f, epsilon=%f'%(delta1, epsilon1), n_points=10**5, color='k')
ax = plt.gca()
In [13]:
grid1.generate_mesh_coverage();
In [14]:
new_grid1 = grid1.mesh_without_intersection(curr_elem1, args='t, delta=%f, epsilon=%f'%(delta1, epsilon1), 
                                            t_min=0.0, t_max=2*np.pi/epsilon1,
                                            min_dist=0.15, n_points=10**4)
In [15]:
fig = plt.figure(figsize=(8,6))
ax = fig.add_subplot(111, projection='3d')
ax.scatter(*new_grid1.T, color='crimson', s=6, alpha=0.8)
ax.set_xlabel('x')
ax.set_ylabel('y')
ax.set_zlabel('z')
ax.view_init(13, 10)

Campo Magnético¶

Forma adimensional do campo magnético

In [16]:
alpha, beta, gamma  = sp.symbols(r'alpha beta gamma', real=True)
In [17]:
position = sp.Matrix([[alpha, beta, gamma]])
In [18]:
dB1 = curr_elem_der1.cross(position-curr_elem1)/(position-curr_elem1).norm()**3
In [19]:
dB1_x = sp.lambdify('t, alpha=1, beta=1, gamma=1, delta=1, epsilon=1', dB1[0])
dB1_y = sp.lambdify('t, alpha=1, beta=1, gamma=1, delta=1, epsilon=1', dB1[1])
dB1_z = sp.lambdify('t, alpha=1, beta=1, gamma=1, delta=1, epsilon=1', dB1[2])

Calculando o campo magnético

In [20]:
b_field1 = MagneticField(new_grid1, [dB1_x, dB1_y, dB1_z], (delta1, epsilon1), tmin=0, tmax=2*np.pi/epsilon1)
In [21]:
field1 = b_field1.get_grid_field();

Visualização¶

In [22]:
field_intensity1 = np.linalg.norm(field1, axis=1)
In [23]:
normalized_field1 = field1/field_intensity1.reshape(-1,1)
In [24]:
colormap1 = generate_colormap(field_intensity1, cmap=plt.cm.Reds)

Linhas de campo¶

In [25]:
fig = plt.figure(figsize=(10,8))
ax = fig.add_subplot(111, projection='3d')
ax.quiver(*new_grid1.T, *normalized_field1.T, colors=colormap1,
          arrow_length_ratio=0.4, length=0.05, pivot='tail')
grid1.plot_curve(curr_elem1, 0, 2*np.pi/epsilon1, args='t, delta=%f, epsilon=%f'%(delta1, epsilon1), ax=ax, n_points=10**5, color='k', linewidth=2)
ax.set_xlabel("$x$")
ax.set_ylabel("$y$")
ax.set_zlabel("$z$")
ax.view_init(10, 30)
plt.savefig('helice.pdf', bbox_inches='tight')
plt.show()

Projeções¶

In [26]:
slicer1 = FieldSlicer(grid1, field1)
In [27]:
projection_axis1 = 2
In [28]:
def animate_projections1(index):
    planes = slicer1.get_valid_planes(projection_axis1)
    coord = planes[index]
    slicer1.plot_projection(coord, projection_axis1, scale=200)
In [29]:
interactive_plot = interactive(animate_projections1, index=IntSlider(min=0, max=len(slicer1.get_valid_planes(projection_axis1))-1,
                                                                     step=1,continuous_update=True, description='Plane Index'))
output = interactive_plot.children[-1]
output.layout.height = '400px'
interactive_plot

Solenoide¶

Gerando a malha¶

In [34]:
grid2 = Grid([-1.2, 1.3, 0.2], [-1.2, 1.2, 0.2], [-0.2, 1.3, 0.1])

Verificando a parametrização¶

In [35]:
delta2, epsilon2 = 1, 0.03
In [36]:
grid2.plot_curve(curr_elem1, 0, 2*np.pi/epsilon2, args='t, delta=%f, epsilon=%f'%(delta2, epsilon2), n_points=10**5, color='k')
ax = plt.gca()
In [37]:
grid2.generate_mesh_coverage();
In [38]:
new_grid2 = grid2.mesh_without_intersection(curr_elem1, args='t, delta=%f, epsilon=%f'%(delta2, epsilon2), t_min=0.0, t_max=2*np.pi/epsilon2, 
                                            min_dist=0.15, n_points=10**4)
In [39]:
fig = plt.figure(figsize=(8,6))
ax = fig.add_subplot(111, projection='3d')
ax.scatter(*new_grid2.T, color='crimson', s=6, alpha=0.8)
ax.set_xlabel('x')
ax.set_ylabel('y')
ax.set_zlabel('z')
ax.view_init(13, 10)

Campo Magnético¶

In [40]:
b_field2 = MagneticField(new_grid2, [dB1_x, dB1_y, dB1_z], (delta2, epsilon2), tmin=0, tmax=2*np.pi/epsilon2)
In [41]:
field2 = b_field2.get_grid_field();

D:\USP\6° Semestre\Eletromagnetismo Computacional\em-comp\project-4\project4.py:176: IntegrationWarning: The maximum number of subdivisions (50) has been achieved.
  If increasing the limit yields no improvement it is advised to analyze 
  the integrand in order to determine the difficulties.  If the position of a 
  local difficulty can be determined (singularity, discontinuity) one will 
  probably gain from splitting up the interval and calling the integrator 
  on the subranges.  Perhaps a special-purpose integrator should be used.
  integral = integrate.quad(comp, self.tmin, self.tmax, args=(alpha, beta, gamma, *self.args))
D:\USP\6° Semestre\Eletromagnetismo Computacional\em-comp\project-4\project4.py:176: IntegrationWarning: The integral is probably divergent, or slowly convergent.
  integral = integrate.quad(comp, self.tmin, self.tmax, args=(alpha, beta, gamma, *self.args))

Visualização¶

In [42]:
field_intensity2 = np.linalg.norm(field2, axis=1)
In [43]:
normalized_field2 = field2/field_intensity2.reshape(-1,1)
In [44]:
colormap2 = generate_colormap(field_intensity2, cmap=plt.cm.Reds)

Linhas de campo¶

In [45]:
fig = plt.figure(figsize=(10,8))
ax = fig.add_subplot(111, projection='3d')
ax.quiver(*new_grid2.T, *normalized_field2.T, colors=colormap2,
          arrow_length_ratio=0.4, length=0.05, pivot='tail')
ax.set_xlabel("$x$")
ax.set_ylabel("$y$")
ax.set_zlabel("$z$")
ax.view_init(10, 30)
#plt.savefig('solenoid.pdf', bbox_inches='tight')
plt.show()

Projeções¶

In [46]:
slicer2 = FieldSlicer(grid2, field2)
In [60]:
projection_axis2 = 2
In [61]:
def animate_projections2(index):
    planes = slicer2.get_valid_planes(projection_axis2)
    coord = planes[index]
    slicer2.plot_projection(coord, projection_axis2,scale=2000)
In [62]:
interactive_plot = interactive(animate_projections2, index=IntSlider(min=0, max=len(slicer2.get_valid_planes(projection_axis2))-1,
                                                                     step=1, continuous_update=True, description='Plane Index'))
output = interactive_plot.children[-1]
output.layout.height = '400px'
interactive_plot

Espira¶

Geração da Malha e Parametrizações¶

Parametrização¶

In [63]:
delta, epsilon = sp.symbols(r'delta epsilon', constants=True)
In [64]:
t = sp.symbols('t', real=True)
In [65]:
curr_elem3 = sp.Matrix([[delta*sp.cos(t), delta*sp.sin(t), epsilon*t/(2*sp.pi)]])
In [66]:
curr_elem_der3 = sp.diff(curr_elem3, t)

Gerando a malha¶

In [67]:
grid3 = Grid([-1.0, 1.1, 0.2], [-1.0, 1.1, 0.2], [-0.5, 0.5, 0.1])

Verificando a parametrização¶

In [68]:
delta3, epsilon3 = 1.0, 0
In [69]:
grid3.plot_curve(curr_elem3, 0, 2*np.pi, args='t, delta=%f, epsilon=%f'%(delta3, epsilon3), n_points=10**4, color='k')
ax = plt.gca()
In [70]:
grid3.generate_mesh_coverage();
In [71]:
new_grid3 = grid3.mesh_without_intersection(curr_elem3, args='t, delta=%f, epsilon=%f'%(delta3, epsilon3), t_min=0.0, t_max=2*np.pi,
                                            min_dist=0.08, n_points=10**4)
In [72]:
fig = plt.figure(figsize=(8,6))
ax = fig.add_subplot(111, projection='3d')
ax.scatter(*new_grid3.T, color='crimson', s=6, alpha=0.8)
ax.set_xlabel('x')
ax.set_ylabel('y')
ax.set_zlabel('z')
ax.view_init(13, 10)

Campo Magnético¶

In [73]:
b_field3 = MagneticField(new_grid3, [dB1_x, dB1_y, dB1_z], (delta3, epsilon3), tmin=0, tmax=2*np.pi)
In [74]:
field3 = b_field3.get_grid_field();

Visualização¶

In [75]:
field_intensity3 = np.linalg.norm(field3, axis=1)
In [76]:
normalized_field3 = field3/field_intensity3.reshape(-1,1)
In [77]:
colormap3 = generate_colormap(field_intensity3, cmap=plt.cm.Reds)

Linhas de campo¶

In [78]:
fig = plt.figure(figsize=(10,8))
ax = fig.add_subplot(111, projection='3d')
ax.quiver(*new_grid3.T, *normalized_field3.T, colors=colormap3,
          arrow_length_ratio=0.4, length=0.05, pivot='tail')
grid3.plot_curve(curr_elem3, 0, 2*np.pi, args='t, delta=%f, epsilon=%f'%(delta3, epsilon3), ax=ax, n_points=10**4,
                 linewidth=2, color='k')
ax.set_xlabel("$x$")
ax.set_ylabel("$y$")
ax.set_zlabel("$z$")
ax.view_init(10, 30)
plt.savefig('espira.pdf', bbox_inches='tight')
plt.show()

Projeções¶

In [79]:
slicer3 = FieldSlicer(grid3, field3)
In [80]:
projection_axis3 = 2
In [81]:
def animate_projections3(index):
    planes = slicer3.get_valid_planes(projection_axis3)
    coord = planes[index]
    slicer3.plot_projection(coord, projection_axis3, scale=80)
In [82]:
interactive_plot = interactive(animate_projections3, index=IntSlider(min=0, max=len(slicer3.get_valid_planes(projection_axis3))-1,
                                                                     step=1, continuous_update=True, description='Plane Index'))
output = interactive_plot.children[-1]
output.layout.height = '400px'
interactive_plot

Fio Finito¶

Parametrização¶

In [83]:
delta, epsilon = sp.symbols(r'delta epsilon', constants=True)
In [84]:
t = sp.symbols('t', real=True)
In [85]:
curr_elem4 = sp.Matrix([[delta*sp.cos(t), delta*sp.sin(t), epsilon*t/(2*sp.pi)]])
In [86]:
curr_elem_der4 = sp.diff(curr_elem4, t)

Gerando a malha¶

In [87]:
grid4 = Grid([-0.05, 0.052, 0.008], [-0.05, 0.05, 0.008], [-0.1, 1.2, 0.1])

Verificando a parametrização¶

In [88]:
delta4, epsilon4 = 0, 1
In [89]:
grid4.plot_curve(curr_elem4, 0, 2*np.pi/epsilon4, args='t, delta=%f, epsilon=%f'%(delta4, epsilon4), n_points=10**5, color='k')
ax = plt.gca()
In [90]:
grid4.generate_mesh_coverage();
In [91]:
new_grid4 = grid4.mesh_without_intersection(curr_elem4, args='t, delta=%f, epsilon=%f'%(delta4, epsilon4), t_min=0.0, t_max=2*np.pi/epsilon4, min_dist=0.01, n_points=10**4)
In [92]:
fig = plt.figure(figsize=(8,6))
ax = fig.add_subplot(111, projection='3d')
ax.scatter(*new_grid4.T, color='crimson', s=6, alpha=0.8)
ax.set_xlabel('x')
ax.set_ylabel('y')
ax.set_zlabel('z')
ax.view_init(13, 10)

Campo Magnético¶

In [93]:
field_4 = MagneticField(new_grid4, [dB1_x, dB1_y, dB1_z], (delta4, epsilon4), tmin=0, tmax=2*np.pi/epsilon4)
In [94]:
field4 = field_4.get_grid_field();

Visualização¶

In [95]:
field_intensity4 = np.linalg.norm(field4, axis=1)
In [96]:
normalized_field4 = field4/field_intensity4.reshape(-1,1)
In [97]:
colormap4 = generate_colormap(field_intensity4, cmap=plt.cm.Reds)

Linhas de campo¶

In [98]:
fig = plt.figure(figsize=(10, 8))
ax = fig.add_subplot(111, projection='3d')
ax.quiver(*new_grid4.T, *normalized_field4.T, colors=colormap4,
          arrow_length_ratio=0.4, length=0.005, pivot='tail')
#grid4.plot_curve(curr_elem4, 0, 2*np.pi/epsilon4, args='t, delta=%f, epsilon=%f'%(delta4, epsilon4), ax=ax, n_points=10**5, color='k')
ax.set_xlabel("$x$")
ax.set_ylabel("$y$")
ax.set_zlabel("$z$")
ax.view_init(70, 30)
plt.savefig('fio.pdf', bbox_inches='tight')
plt.show()

Projeções¶

In [103]:
slicer4 = FieldSlicer(grid4,field4)
In [104]:
projection_axis4 = 2
In [105]:
def animate_projections4(index):
    planes = slicer4.get_valid_planes(projection_axis4)
    coord = planes[index]
    slicer4.plot_projection(coord, projection_axis4, scale=4000)
In [106]:
interactive_plot = interactive(animate_projections4, index=IntSlider(min=0, max=len(slicer4.get_valid_planes(projection_axis4))-1,
                                                                     step=1, continuous_update=True, description='Plane Index'))
output = interactive_plot.children[-1]
output.layout.height = '400px'
interactive_plot

Toroide¶

Geração da Malha e Parametrizações¶

Parametrização¶

In [107]:
r, s = sp.symbols(r'rho sigma', constants=True)
In [108]:
t = sp.symbols('t', real=True)
In [109]:
curr_elem5 = sp.Matrix([[(r + sp.cos(s*t))*sp.cos(t), (r + sp.cos(s*t))*sp.sin(t), sp.sin(s*t)]])
In [110]:
curr_elem_der5 = sp.diff(curr_elem5, t)

Gerando a malha¶

In [90]:
grid5 = Grid([-2.1, 2.2, .35], [-2.1, 2.2, .35], [-1.3, 1.4, 0.2])

Verificando a parametrização¶

In [91]:
rho, sigma = 2, 100
In [92]:
grid5.plot_curve(curr_elem5, 0, 2*np.pi, args='t, rho=%f, sigma=%f'%(rho, sigma), n_points=10**4, color='k')
ax = plt.gca()
In [93]:
grid5.generate_mesh_coverage();
In [94]:
new_grid5 = grid5.mesh_without_intersection(curr_elem5, args='t, rho=%f, sigma=%f'%(rho, sigma), t_min=0.0, 
                                            t_max=2*np.pi, min_dist=0.15, n_points=10**4)
In [95]:
fig = plt.figure(figsize=(8,6))
ax = fig.add_subplot(111, projection='3d')
ax.scatter(*new_grid5.T, color='crimson', s=6, alpha=0.8)
ax.set_xlabel('x')
ax.set_ylabel('y')
ax.set_zlabel('z')
ax.view_init(13, 10)

Campo Magnético¶

Forma adimensional do campo magnético

Definindo as seguintes variáveis adimensionais:

$$\rho = \frac{R}{r}\quad \sigma = k\quad $$
In [96]:
alpha, beta, gamma  = sp.symbols(r'alpha beta gamma', real=True)
In [97]:
position = sp.Matrix([[alpha, beta, gamma]])
In [98]:
dB5 = curr_elem_der5.cross(position-curr_elem5)/(position-curr_elem5).norm()**3
In [99]:
dB5_x = sp.lambdify('t, alpha=1, beta=1, gamma=1, rho=1, sigma=1', dB5[0])
dB5_y = sp.lambdify('t, alpha=1, beta=1, gamma=1, rho=1, sigma=1', dB5[1])
dB5_z = sp.lambdify('t, alpha=1, beta=1, gamma=1, rho=1, sigma=1', dB5[2])
In [100]:
b_field5 = MagneticField(new_grid5, [dB5_x, dB5_y, dB5_z], (rho, sigma), tmin=0, tmax=2*np.pi)
In [101]:
field5 = b_field5.get_grid_field();

D:\USP\6° Semestre\Eletromagnetismo Computacional\em-comp\project-4\project4.py:176: IntegrationWarning: The integral is probably divergent, or slowly convergent.
  integral = integrate.quad(comp, self.tmin, self.tmax, args=(alpha, beta, gamma, *self.args))
D:\USP\6° Semestre\Eletromagnetismo Computacional\em-comp\project-4\project4.py:176: IntegrationWarning: The maximum number of subdivisions (50) has been achieved.
  If increasing the limit yields no improvement it is advised to analyze 
  the integrand in order to determine the difficulties.  If the position of a 
  local difficulty can be determined (singularity, discontinuity) one will 
  probably gain from splitting up the interval and calling the integrator 
  on the subranges.  Perhaps a special-purpose integrator should be used.
  integral = integrate.quad(comp, self.tmin, self.tmax, args=(alpha, beta, gamma, *self.args))

Visualização¶

In [102]:
field_intensity5 = np.linalg.norm(field5, axis=1)
In [103]:
normalized_field5 = field5/field_intensity5.reshape(-1,1)
In [104]:
colormap5 = generate_colormap(field_intensity5, cmap=plt.cm.Reds)

Linhas de campo¶

In [105]:
fig = plt.figure(figsize=(8,6))
ax = fig.add_subplot(111, projection='3d')
ax.quiver(*new_grid5.T, *normalized_field5.T, colors=colormap5, 
          arrow_length_ratio=0.4, length=0.2, pivot='tail', zorder=-1)
ax.set_xlabel("$x$")
ax.set_ylabel("$y$")
ax.set_zlabel("$z$")
ax.view_init(45, 40)
plt.savefig('toroid.pdf', bbox_inches='tight')
plt.show()

Projeções¶

In [106]:
slicer5 = FieldSlicer(grid5, field5)
In [110]:
projection_axis5 = 2
In [111]:
def animate_projections5(index):
    planes = slicer5.get_valid_planes(projection_axis5)
    coord = planes[index]
    slicer5.plot_projection(coord, projection_axis5, scale=2000)
In [112]:
interactive_plot = interactive(animate_projections5, index=IntSlider(min=0, max=len(slicer5.get_valid_planes(projection_axis5))-1,
                                                                     step=1, continuous_update=True, description='Plane Index'))
output = interactive_plot.children[-1]
output.layout.height = '400px'
interactive_plot
In [ ]:
